moves.note: Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero
Cut all leveraged ETFs to zero

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and comply with the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce SMH to 5% due to its downtrend and to de-risk.
Increase QQQI to 25% for its income focus and stability.
Buy XLV at 5% to diversify into a quality, less correlated sector.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall exposure.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month by prioritizing capital preservation and de-risking. It aims for low drawdown by cutting leveraged ETFs and reducing concentration. Expect slower growth but enhanced stability.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and manage overall exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure to zero.
Reduce SMH to 8% to lower risk in a downtrending sector.
Buy LRCX at 5% as a high-scoring semiconductor setup.
Increase QQQI to 20% to maintain a balance of income and growth.
This plan keeps a moderate cash buffer and balances risk with growth.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while blending broad index with quality tech. Expect moderate growth with managed drawdown.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL to exit the flagged position and reallocate capital.
Reduce SMH to 5% to cut exposure to the downtrending sector.
Buy LRCX at 10% and ASML at 10% to concentrate in high-scoring semiconductor setups.
Watch TXN for a potential 5% buy, leveraging strong scanner signals.
This plan uses a smaller cash buffer and leans into high-conviction setups.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth by concentrating in high-scoring setups. It embraces higher risk and potential for significant drawdown due to concentration and leveraged exposure. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage losses.

moves.note: Cut all leveraged and high-beta ETFs
Cut all leveraged and high-beta ETFs

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and manage overall risk.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Reduce CRWD to 5% to lower high-beta single name exposure.
Sell SMH to exit the downtrending, high-beta semiconductor sector.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize exposure to income-focused ETFs.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month by rotating into income-focused ETFs and broad index. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth, keeping a cash buffer. Distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in selloffs.
